## What is Unthread?

Unthread is an AI-powered help desk that lives entirely inside Slack. It automatically converts Slack messages in designated support channels into tracked tickets — no separate portal, no context switching. Customers and employees file requests in Slack the same way they always have; Unthread captures, routes, and resolves them behind the scenes.

## Who is it for?

Unthread serves IT teams, HR departments, RevOps teams, and customer support organizations that use Slack as their primary collaboration layer. It's used by fast-growing startups and enterprises alike (customers include Intuit, Lemonade, and Automattic) that want to stop losing track of requests buried in channel threads.

## Strengths

- **Zero-friction intake** — no new portal for users; tickets are created automatically from Slack messages.
- **Agentic AI** — AI agents resolve approximately 40% of tickets automatically without human intervention.
- **Self-learning knowledge base** — AI drafts and updates knowledge base articles from resolved tickets.
- **SLA and on-call tracking** — built-in SLO monitoring and on-call rotation management.
- **G2 rating** — holds a 4.9/5 rating on G2 with strong reviews from engineering and IT teams.

## What to consider

- Pricing ranges from $50–$250/agent/mo, which can be expensive at scale.
- Limited value for teams that don't use Slack as a primary communication layer.
- Some advanced features like omni-channel (email, portal) require higher tiers.

## Bottom line

Unthread is the most polished Slack-native help desk available. If your team and customers already live in Slack, the friction savings alone justify the cost — you get a real ticketing system without forcing anyone to change how they work.
